messages.content: Create a 3-day vegetarian meal plan for weight loss under 1500 calories per day.

Day 1: Breakfast - Greek yogurt 1 cup, mixed berries 1/2 cup, honey 1 tsp, chopped nuts 1 tbsp; Lunch - Quinoa salad with cherry tomatoes, cucumber, feta, spinach; Dinner - Stir-fried tofu with mixed vegetables and brown rice; Snacks - Baby carrots with hummus, Apple with almonds. Day 2: Breakfast - Oatmeal 1/2 cup, almond milk 1 cup, banana, chia seeds 1 tbsp; Lunch - Whole grain wrap with black beans, avocado, lettuce, tomato, salsa; Dinner - Stuffed bell peppers with brown rice, black beans, cheese. Snacks - Greek yogurt with strawberries, Almonds. Day 3: Breakfast - Spinach and banana smoothie with almond milk and peanut butter; Lunch - Lentil soup with whole grain roll; Dinner - Cauliflower rice stir-fry with tofu and mixed vegetables; Snacks - Mixed nuts, Pear.
Explain the ideal macronutrient ratio for muscle gain in simple terms.

For muscle gain: Protein 25-30% (helps build muscle), Carbohydrates 45-60% (provides energy), Fats 15-25% (supports hormones and energy). Example for 2500 calories: Protein 188g, Carbs 313g, Fats 56g. Adjust based on your body weight and activity level.
Adjust this 3-day meal plan for a gluten-free user.

Replace all gluten-containing items with gluten-free alternatives: Quinoa or rice instead of bread/wraps, gluten-free oats, gluten-free pasta. Ensure sauces and broths are certified gluten-free. Maintain similar calorie and macronutrient distribution.
Give me a motivational message for someone trying to stick to a healthy diet.
Remember, every small choice counts! Celebrate each healthy meal, stay consistent, and focus on progress, not perfection. You've got this!
Summarize the key benefits of a Mediterranean diet in under 100 words.

The Mediterranean diet supports heart health with healthy fats, reduces inflammation with fruits, vegetables, and whole grains, balances macronutrients, is high in fiber, limits processed foods, and provides essential vitamins and minerals. It aids in weight management and may improve mental health, promoting longevity and overall well-being.
Create a 5-day vegetarian meal plan for muscle gain.

Day 1: Breakfast - Oatmeal with protein powder, banana; Lunch - Quinoa salad with chickpeas, avocado, spinach; Dinner - Tofu stir-fry with brown rice; Snacks - Greek yogurt, almonds. Day 2: Breakfast - Smoothie with spinach, peanut butter, almond milk; Lunch - Lentil soup, gluten-free bread; Dinner - Veggie chili with beans; Snacks - Cottage cheese, fruit. Day 3: ... (continue similarly for Days 3-5).
Provide a sample meal plan for someone on a ketogenic diet.

Breakfast - Scrambled eggs with spinach and cheese; Lunch - Grilled chicken salad with olive oil dressing; Dinner - Baked salmon with asparagus; Snacks - Almonds, cheese cubes. Ensure net carbs are under 30g per day.
Explain how to balance macronutrients for weight loss.

For weight loss: Protein 30-35% (supports muscle retention), Carbs 35-40% (fuel with moderation), Fats 25-30% (satiety and hormones). Focus on whole foods, portion control, and a slight calorie deficit.
Create a 3-day gluten-free Mediterranean meal plan.

Day 1: Breakfast - Greek yogurt with honey and berries; Lunch - Quinoa salad with vegetables, feta, olive oil; Dinner - Baked salmon with roasted vegetables; Snacks - Nuts, fruit. Day 2: ... (similar pattern for Days 2-3).
Provide tips for healthy snacking.

Choose snacks high in protein and fiber like Greek yogurt, nuts, fruits, veggies with hummus. Avoid sugary, processed snacks. Portion your snacks to prevent overeating and maintain balanced energy throughout the day.
Explain why protein is important for muscle recovery.

Protein provides amino acids necessary for repairing and building muscle tissue after exercise. Adequate protein intake reduces muscle soreness, supports growth, and helps maintain muscle mass during calorie deficits.
Give advice for someone who wants to start intermittent fasting.

Start gradually by shortening your eating window, stay hydrated, focus on nutrient-dense foods, and monitor energy levels. Combine with balanced meals to ensure adequate protein, healthy fats, and fiber during eating periods.
Provide a vegetarian high-protein meal plan for 2000 calories.

Breakfast - Protein smoothie with pea protein, banana, almond milk; Lunch - Lentil salad with chickpeas, quinoa, and vegetables; Dinner - Tofu stir-fry with broccoli, peppers, and brown rice; Snacks - Greek yogurt, almonds, boiled eggs.
Offer advice for increasing daily vegetable intake.

Include vegetables in every meal: add spinach to smoothies, include salads with lunch/dinner, snack on carrot sticks or bell pepper slices, roast mixed vegetables as sides. Aim for 5+ servings per day.
Generate a 3-day meal plan for someone with diabetes.

Day 1: Breakfast - Oatmeal with chia seeds and berries; Lunch - Grilled chicken salad with olive oil dressing; Dinner - Baked salmon with quinoa and steamed broccoli; Snacks - Nuts, Greek yogurt. Focus on low glycemic index carbs and fiber. Day 2-3: similar balanced pattern.
Create a high-protein vegan meal plan.
Breakfast - Tofu scramble with vegetables; Lunch - Quinoa salad with chickpeas, kidney beans, and vegetables; Dinner - Lentil stew with brown rice; Snacks - Edamame, nuts, protein smoothie.
Explain how to modify meals for someone allergic to nuts.
Replace all nuts with seeds (sunflower, pumpkin), soy products, or legumes. Ensure packaged foods are nut-free. Keep similar protein and fat content with alternative sources.
Provide a motivational tip for someone struggling to eat vegetables.
Start small: add one extra vegetable per meal, mix them into favorite dishes, and try new recipes weekly. Celebrate small wins and track progress.
Give a 3-day meal plan suitable for a paleo diet.

Day 1: Breakfast - Scrambled eggs with spinach; Lunch - Grilled chicken salad with olive oil; Dinner - Baked salmon with roasted vegetables; Snacks - Fruits, boiled eggs. Days 2-3: Continue with lean meats, vegetables, fruits, nuts.
Explain why hydration is important in a nutrition plan.

Water supports digestion, nutrient transport, body temperature regulation, and exercise performance. Adequate hydration aids metabolism and can help control hunger, supporting overall nutrition goals.
